description_long: This is a military-style jet airplane with a slender fuselage and sharply swept-back wings, painted primarily in a weathered light gray with some green accents on the tail and nose. The aircraft features a pointed nose cone, likely housing radar or avionics, and a single-seat cockpit positioned near the front of the fuselage with a clear canopy. The wings are mid-mounted and sharply swept, with no visible winglets, and the tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear is extended, showing a tricycle configuration with two main wheels and a single nose wheel. The engine intake is located at the nose, suggesting a single jet engine configuration. The overall appearance is that of a classic Cold War-era fighter jet, robust and built for speed and maneuverability.
description: A weathered, gray military jet airplane with swept-back mid-mounted wings, a pointed nose, and a single-seat cockpit near the front. The aircraft has a tricycle landing gear and a single vertical tail fin.
description_view_0: The airplane is seen from a high front angle, highlighting the pointed nose, cockpit canopy, and the swept-back wings.
description_view_1: A clear side profile shows the full length of the fuselage, the tricycle landing gear, and the single vertical stabilizer at the tail.
description_view_2: A high rear view reveals the tail section, horizontal stabilizers, and the overall wing sweep, with the engine exhaust visible at the back.
description_view_3: The opposite side profile displays the landing gear, cockpit, and the angular design of the wings and tail.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, sharply swept wings with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single jet engine with nose intake
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle, retractable landing gear with two main wheels and one nose wheel
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy
